prompt: Show me any emails from today that need a reply.

failures: judge email_operator score 3 < 4: The answer shows some business judgment by separating a likely personal reply candidate from items that are already delegated, informational, or only worth watching. However, it fails the core request because it admits today’s mail is not actually synced, so it cannot reliably answer “emails from today that need a reply.” It also includes several thread-specific claims that may be accurate but are not grounded in a trustworthy today-only lookup, which weakens evidence handling.
score.finalText:
Nothing from **today** is actually synced yet.

The Outlook sync for **bclymer@alleatogroup.com** is stale — last completed **May 18 at 1:24 PM ET** — so I can’t trust “today” as fully checked. What I do have is the **latest available synced inbox**, and from that set these are the threads that looked like they needed attention before they were answered or delegated:

- **Jonathon Knapp — “Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp”**  
  This looked like a reply candidate if you wanted to keep the recruiting conversation warm. He sent a thoughtful follow-up after your conversation.  
  Status from what I can see: **waiting on you / optional reply**.

- **Danville Theater Apt - Invoice — Jerome Daksiewicz**  
  Jerome asked who to send the invoice to and what the structural scope covered.  
  Status from what I can see: **already handed to Jesse**, and Jerome replied that the structural work was for adding loft apartments. So this is **not sitting on you anymore**.

- **Hillsdale-Alleato A141 — Logan Stevens / legal thread**  
  Logan followed up with opposing counsel and was pushing contract comments forward.  
  Status from what I can see: **active legal thread**, but not clearly waiting on you at this moment. More **watch** than immediate reply.

- **Exol Wilmer Drywall bid proposal — AZ Interiors**  
  New bid came in with attachment.  
  Status: **review/delegate**, not necessarily reply yourself.

- **McLane Jazz Rack - Pay Application / Commitment in JobPlanner — Catherine Leopold**  
  She said billing wasn’t showing in JobPlanner and needed help before the deadline.  
  Status: **needs team response**, likely Kebba/accounting/ops more than you personally.

Everything else near the top of the synced inbox was either:
- already resolved,
- informational,
- marketing/spam,
- system alerts,
- or something you had already forwarded/delegated.

Bottom line: from the latest synced mail, the only clear **personal reply candidate** was **Jon Knapp**. The others are mostly **delegate/watch**, not “Brandon must reply now.”

If you want, I can do one of these next:
1. **Draft a reply to Jon Knapp**
2. **Pull the full thread for Hillsdale A141 and tell you if legal is waiting on you**
3. **Check Outlook sync health/live status** so we know why today’s inbox is behind

judge.summary: The answer shows some business judgment by separating a likely personal reply candidate from items that are already delegated, informational, or only worth watching. However, it fails the core request because it admits today’s mail is not actually synced, so it cannot reliably answer “emails from today that need a reply.” It also includes several thread-specific claims that may be accurate but are not grounded in a trustworthy today-only lookup, which weakens evidence handling.
strengths: Separates urgent-ish items from routine noise instead of dumping the inbox.
Identifies likely action status for several threads: reply, delegate, watch, or already handled.
Avoids falsely claiming that it checked today's inbox and explicitly flags sync staleness.
Does not claim an email was sent.
weaknesses: Does not truly satisfy the prompt because it cannot confirm emails from today.
Leans on stale inbox data, so the recommendations are not tied to the requested time window.
Some thread summaries are a bit verbose and include unnecessary narrative detail.
The conclusion is hedged heavily and does not give a crisp actionable list for today.
No concrete response path is provided for each item in a concise operator format; the 'draft' option is offered only as a next step, not applied to the identified email.

emails.subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
emails.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
Date: 2026-05-18T16:19:04Z
From: Jonathon Knapp <jonathon.knapp@icloud.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

You don't often get email from jonathon.knapp@icloud.com. Learn why this is important Brandon, Thank you again for taking the time to connect this morning. I appreciated the opportunity to learn more about your vision for the company and the direction the team is heading. I also appreciated the direct conversation around design-build experience. While my background has been rooted more heavily in large-scale renovation, exterior construction, and operational leadership, I believe many of the same fundamentals translate - building strong teams, driving accountability, managing risk, and executing work efficiently at scale. Additionally, my reconstruction and remediation background has provided extensive exposure to building failures, envelope issues, and long-term performance concerns. I believe that perspective can bring value to new construction environments by helping identify and mitigate risk early in the process, while also supporting any future expansion into reconstruction or renovation-related work. I appreciate your time and consideration and enjoyed the conversation. Thank you, Jon Knapp 303.918.2540

Based on the recent impact and leadership presence, we would love to shortlist “Brandon Clymer” for the Lead Cover Story of this edition.   This feature is not just about visibility but about sharing the journey behind the success—the vision, experiences, challenges, and leadership perspective—with our global audience of founders, business leaders, and CXOs.   This collaboration includes:   Lead Cover Feature with a 6–8 pages in-depth profile (Print + Digital) Leadership Viewpoint opportunities in upcoming editions Digital promotion across our social media platforms Do-follow backlink for long-term digital visibility Official edition logos, Certificate of Honor, and brand assets Two full-page ad placements for announcements or campaigns Press release and announcement publishing in our Partner Section Sponsorship: USD 500   If this sounds of interest, I would be happy to share more details and the next steps. We would be delighted to include your story in this special edition.   We look forward to hearing your thoughts. Thanks & Regards, Daniel Robles Global Business Development Manager The Prime Today Magazine Email ID: Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com

emails.subject: Fw: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine
emails.bodyText:
Subject: Fw: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine
Date: 2026-05-18T16:05:42Z
From: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>
To: Maria Calcetero <Mcalcetero@alleatogroup.com>

Brandon Clymer CEO at Alleato Group  Mobile   317.760.0088   |   Email   bclymer@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com     |         Indianapolis - 8383 Craig Street, Suite 150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  Tampa/St Pete - 701 94th Avenue North, Suite 118, St. Petersburg, FL 33702     From: Daniel Robles <Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com> Sent: Monday, May 18, 2026 8:53 AM To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Subject: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine   Dear Brandon,   I hope you’re doing well.   I’ve been following the work “Brandon Clymer”  has been doing, and I truly appreciate the impact and leadership approach—it really stands out and reflects meaningful work being done.   I’m reaching out because we are currently working on a special edition of The Prime Today Magazine , titled  “Top 10 Pioneering Leaders Driving Food Industry Innovation & Restaurant Business Growth for 2026.”  This edition is focused on authentic leadership stories—going beyond traditional profiles to highlight leaders who are making a real difference. Based on the recent impact and leadership presence, we would love to shortlist “Brandon Clymer” for the Lead Cover Story of this edition.   This feature is not just about visibility but about sharing the journey behind the success—the vision, experiences, challenges, and leadership perspective—with our global audience of founders, business leaders, and CXOs.   This collaboration includes:   Lead Cover Feature with a 6–8 pages in-depth profile (Print + Digital) Leadership Viewpoint opportunities in upcoming editions Digital promotion across our social media platforms Do-follow backlink for long-term digital visibility Official edition logos, Certificate of Honor, and brand assets Two full-page ad placements for announcements or campaigns Press release and announcement publishing in our Partner Section Sponsorship: USD 500   If this sounds of interest, I would be happy to share more details and the next steps. We would be delighted to include your story in this special edition.   We look forward to hearing your thoughts. Thanks & Regards, Daniel Robles Global Business Development Manager The Prime Today Magazine Email ID: Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com

Subject: RE: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1,  Revit Models
Date: 2026-05-18T15:12:54Z
From: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>
To: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>, Vinod Ravikumar <vinod.ravikumar@exol.com>, Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>, Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>, Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>, Bob  Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>, Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>, Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>, Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>, Robert Snedeker <rsnedeker@symbotic.com>

You don't often get email from wblank@symbotic.com. Learn why this is important Hello Steve,   Please find the reaction data attached.  I’ll post them on the SharePoint drive also.    The column separation is 4“x4” lateral and 5.3” vertical.   Spanning between the slab control joints is typical.     WILLIAM BLANK | senior project engineer e: wblank@symbotic.com www.symbotic.com SYMBOTIC 200 Research Drive Wilmington, MA 01887 Reinvent the warehouse | Reimagine the supply chain® **Symbotic Confidential** This e-mail and any attachments contain Symbotic confidential information and are for the sole use of the intended recipient(s). Unauthorized review, use, or disclosure is prohibited. If you are not the intended recipient, please contact the sender by reply e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message.   From: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com> Sent: Friday, May 15, 2026 2:56 PM To: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>; Vinod Ravikumar <vinod.ravikumar@exol.com>; Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>; Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>; Bob Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>; Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>; Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>; Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>; Robert Snedeker <rsnedeker@symbotic.com> Cc: Connor Brulotte <cbrulotte@symbotic.com>; Mark Jodoin <mjodoin@symbotic.com>; Donald Seifert <dseifert@symbotic.com>; Michael Cole <mcole@symbotic.com>; Alex Calder <acalder@symbotic.com>; Andrew Grandin <agrandin@symbotic.com>; Parker Asmus <parker.asmus@exol.com> Subject: RE: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1, Revit Models   CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the Symbotic organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content. Please report any suspicious emails to Symbotic IT Helpdesk.   Hello All,   I just got off a call with the Morrisville landlord to review our first phase of plans. They have a concern regarding the pad that is located in close proximity to column K19 based on how close it would be to the footer for that building column. @SYM team Is there a distance we typically try and keep away from these columns for concrete work, this does look very tight?   @Jerome Daksiewicz @Bob Wright @Brandon Clymer we need to have the structural engineer look at this more closely.   I also want to understand if we feel this is a fully inclusive list of pads needed for the slab. There doesn’t appear to be that many compared to some previous projects. Do we feel comfortable that this is all-inclusive or think there could be more coming?   DKGR/Alleato Team, have we assessed some of the areas called out on the pits&pads drawing to be confirmed by EOR (ex. #9 to be confirmed as not needed) We also NEED the reactions details for these ASAP – the landlord basically said there is nothing for them to review without having the structural reactions for these. @William Blank @Robert Snedeker what is the ETA for these? Finally, they did call out that some of these pits & pads locations appear to span across control joints in the slab and suggested trying to avoid that. I said I would pass it along, but told them this is most likely more difficult and less flexible than the column footer interference. There are less control joints on this slab being a Trilytics Trislab, but they called out that because there are less control joints, there is potentially more of a shift on them.   System Designs — Trilytics   We should probably discuss on a call early next week.   Steve Fischer Senior Director, Facilities M: +1 610-764-3984 The Robotic Logistics Platform ™ built for tomorrow. | www.exol.com   From: William Blank < wblank@symbotic.com > Sent: Thursday, May 7, 2026 10:22 AM To: Steve Fischer < steve.fischer@exol.com >; emails.bodyText:
Subject: Cover Story Feature for Brandon Clymer in The Prime Today Magazine
Date: 2026-05-18T12:53:40Z
From: Daniel Robles <Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Dear Brandon,   I hope you’re doing well.   I’ve been following the work “Brandon Clymer”  has been doing, and I truly appreciate the impact and leadership approach—it really stands out and reflects meaningful work being done.   I’m reaching out because we are currently working on a special edition of The Prime Today Magazine , titled  “Top 10 Pioneering Leaders Driving Food Industry Innovation & Restaurant Business Growth for 2026.”  This edition is focused on authentic leadership stories—going beyond traditional profiles to highlight leaders who are making a real difference. Based on the recent impact and leadership presence, we would love to shortlist “Brandon Clymer” for the Lead Cover Story of this edition.   This feature is not just about visibility but about sharing the journey behind the success—the vision, experiences, challenges, and leadership perspective—with our global audience of founders, business leaders, and CXOs.   This collaboration includes:   Lead Cover Feature with a 6–8 pages in-depth profile (Print + Digital) Leadership Viewpoint opportunities in upcoming editions Digital promotion across our social media platforms Do-follow backlink for long-term digital visibility Official edition logos, Certificate of Honor, and brand assets Two full-page ad placements for announcements or campaigns Press release and announcement publishing in our Partner Section Sponsorship: USD 500   If this sounds of interest, I would be happy to share more details and the next steps. We would be delighted to include your story in this special edition.   We look forward to hearing your thoughts. Thanks & Regards, Daniel Robles Global Business Development Manager The Prime Today Magazine Email ID: Daniel.Robles@theprimetoday.com

Good morning, Grant. Checking in here. Thanks!   Logan  Stevens ,  Associate ​ ​ ​ ​ Mobile:  (574) 606-6689   |   Direct:  (260) 425-4742 |  Email:  Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com 888 S. Harrison Street Suite 600, Fort Wayne, IN 46802 Atlanta | Baltimore | Boston | Chicago | Dallas | Delaware | Denver | Florida | Indiana | Los Angeles | Michigan | Minneapolis Nashville | New Jersey | New York | Ohio | Philadelphia | Phoenix | Raleigh | Salt Lake City | San Diego | Washington, D.C.   From: Logan Stevens Sent: Thursday, May 14, 2026 2:21 PM To: 'Schertzing, Grant E.' <schertzingg@millerjohnson.com> Cc: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Mark Ferderer <mferderer@ferdimation.com> Subject: RE: Hillsdale-Alleato A141   Grant,   A few comments. I think we are close. 3.14 – As we discussed on the call, the concern stems around the increase to contract sum and contract time if Hillsdale rejects the proposed subs.   If the parties are going to contract now with the proposal, any changes resulting from Hillsdale rejecting Alleato’s current selections will not be accounted for. If Hillsdale rejects Alleato’s subs that have been selected, they should not be on the hook for increased costs arising from the rejection. So we maintain this language should remain. 3.1.14.1 – Indemnification. Alleato will agree to these; however, the indemnification should not include “breach of contract” if we are not limiting to third party claims. This would create an unusually broad indemnity. It should be either/or. We can leave third party claims or breach of contract but not both. If you can accommodate our requests above, Alleato agrees to all other changes. Feel free to give me a call to discuss. I am free until 4 pm eastern. Thanks!   Logan  Stevens ,  Associate ​ ​ ​ ​ Mobile:  (574) 606-6689   |   Direct:  (260) 425-4742 |  Email:  Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com 888 S. Harrison Street Suite 600, Fort Wayne, IN 46802 Atlanta | Baltimore | Boston | Chicago | Dallas | Delaware | Denver | Florida | Indiana | Los Angeles | Michigan | Minneapolis Nashville | New Jersey | New York | Ohio | Philadelphia | Phoenix | Raleigh | Salt Lake City | San Diego | Washington, D.C.   From: Schertzing, Grant E. < schertzingg@millerjohnson.com > Sent: Thursday, May 14, 2026 10:22 AM To: Logan Stevens < Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com > Cc: Brandon Clymer < bclymer@alleatogroup.com >; Mark Ferderer < mferderer@ferdimation.com > Subject: [EXTERNAL] RE: Hillsdale-Alleato A141   Logan, Thank you for talking through a few points this morning. emails.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Exol - Morrisville PA - Weekly Status Call - Exol/Alleato
Date: 2026-05-18T12:51:04Z
From: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>
To: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>, Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>, Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>, Parker Asmus <parker.asmus@exol.com>, Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>, Kebba Mass <kmass@alleatogroup.com>, Bob  Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>, Behrad Shahlaee <behrad.shahlaee@exol.com>, Enzo Andrade <eandrade@alleatogroup.com>

Steve, We are happy to use the same contractor, please request contact information of that company from them.  If they have any close out documents from the original build we can make sure to reach out to as many of the original contractors as possible to keep continuity.  I will get with the team to get all the structural information we can currently into the LL.  Thank You  Brandon Clymer CEO at Alleato Group  Mobile   317.760.0088   |   Email   bclymer@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com     |         Indianapolis - 8383 Craig Street, Suite 150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  Tampa/St Pete - 701 94th Avenue North, Suite 118, St. Petersburg, FL 33702     From: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com> Sent: Friday, May 15, 2026 3:00 PM To: Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>; Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>; Parker Asmus <parker.asmus@exol.com>; Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>; Kebba Mass <kmass@alleatogroup.com>; Bob Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>; Behrad Shahlaee <behrad.shahlaee@exol.com>; Enzo Andrade <eandrade@alleatogroup.com> Cc: Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>; Jake Richardson <jrichardson@alleatogroup.com> Subject: RE: Exol - Morrisville PA - Weekly Status Call - Exol/Alleato   Hi All,   The landlord has requested structural details for any roof supporting items in our plans (electric, pneumatic, etc.) and any of the structure/slab items - I know we are waiting on Symbotic for this, but we need to put together any other details we can while we wait on their feedback.   They also asked that we ensure we use John Mansville products for the roof applicable to keep the warranty – I told them we will use the shell roofing contractor to keep things simple there with the warranty, and they said they can have John Mansville do an inspection post roof penetration and ensure everything is compliance. Let’s make note of this for after we complete some of that work.   Steve Fischer Senior Director, Facilities M: +1 610-764-3984 The Robotic Logistics Platform ™ built for tomorrow. | www.exol.com   -----Original Appointment----- From: Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com> Sent: Thursday, March 19, 2026 10:53 AM To: Heather Jutras; Brandon Clymer; Jerome Daksiewicz; Parker Asmus; Parker Hollingsworth; Steve Fischer; Kebba Mass; Bob Wright; Behrad Shahlaee; Enzo Andrade Cc: Scott Thomas; Jake Richardson Subject: Exol - Morrisville PA - Weekly Status Call - Exol/Alleato When: Tuesday, May 12, 2026 3:00 PM-4:00 PM (UTC-05:00) Eastern Time (US & Canada). Where: Microsoft Teams Meeting   Weekly project team touch base for Exol’s Morrisville, PA site.   Additional project stakeholders to be added as we move through each phase of the project.     ________________________________________________________________________________ Microsoft Teams meeting Join: https://teams.microsoft.com/meet/24008798375917?p=npGJguG2x9n4PgTCSY Meeting ID: 240 087 983 759 17 Passcode: KZ6mZ7gr Need help? | System reference Dial in by phone +1 469-214-9442,,203058379# United States, Palmer Find a local number Phone conference ID: 203 058 379# For organizers: Meeting options | Reset dial-in PIN ________________________________________________________________________________

Can't read or see images? View this email in a browser. RESCHEDULED: Hawthorns 500 Join us on Saturday May 30th for the first Saturday Men’s Club event of the season with the Hawthorns 500 Flag tournament.  Please see the flyer below for all the details and click the link to get registered. REGISTER ▶ Callaway Fitting Day Callaway will be here at Hawthorns for a fitting day on Wednesday May 20th from 2:00-6:00 with the new Quantum product.  Schedule your fitting below to upgrade your equipment for the season! REGISTER ▶ Titleist GTS Fitting Day Titleist will be here on Tuesday June 9 th for a fitting day with the brand-new GTS Metal release.  This will be a great opportunity to test the newest driver on the market for 2026.  Over 50 tour players have switched to the GTS models.  Click the link to book your fitting! REGISTER ▶ Pro’s Fore Clothes With the month of May comes our annual trade-in trade-up golf shirt sale! From now until the end of the month we are looking to donate as much clothing as possible to the Pros Fore Clothes Foundation! The Pros Fore Clothes Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit foundation that provides clothing to people in need within the United States and around the world. This foundation has donated over 350,000 pounds of clothing and The Hawthorns Golf Staff is proud to be an integral part of their success! Please see below for pricing and promotional details. Donate 1 shirt and receive 15% off 1 new shirt in stock Donate 2 shirts and receive 20% off 2 new shirts in stock Donate 3 shirts and receive 25% off 3 new shirts in stock *The sale is also eligible for shorts and pants!   Junior Golf June Sessions Our Hawthorns Junior Golf June sessions are right around the corner with Little Linksters for ages 3-5 and the Junior Development Program for ages 6-12 starting on Tuesday June 9 th .  Please see the flyer below for all the details and click the link to register your child.  REGISTER FOR LITTLE LINKSTERS REGISTER FOR JUNIOR PROGRAM Ladies Wednesday Evening 9 & Wine Ladies, please join us this season on Wednesday evenings starting on May 20 th for a fun night on the golf course!  We will have wine outside the golf shop from 5:15-5:45 p.m. and then shotgun on the course from 5:45-7:45 p.m.  We encourage everyone to stay after the round and join the group in the Players Lounge Area for drinks, dinner, and social period.  This is a great way to learn the game and meet other ladies in a FUN, stress free environment! You can make your own pairings each week, just be sure to register by 12:00 p.m. each Wednesday.  If you do not have a group, the golf shop will be happy to pair you up!  You will find links to each week on the Calendar of the Hawthorns Website. CLICK HERE REGISTER 2026 Golf Schedule There are many exciting events on the schedule for 2026!  You can also find the golf events on the calendar of the Hawthorns website. CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE 2026 GOLF SCHEDULE Fairways & Greens, Philip Adams, PGA Director of Golf The Hawthorns Golf & Country Club The Hawthorns Golf & Country Club 12255 Club Point Drive Fishers, IN 46037 317.845.9100 | www.hawthornscountryclub.com Unsubscribe from Hawthorns Communications

He's sending a resume and project list that I'll forward once I have it. He's happy at Thompson Thrift but starting to look for other opportunities recently.  When they hired him 3 years ago they needed him to come on board and change things, they had some people leave and some bad projects and they needed someone to lead the division and precon, etc. which he really enjoyed about it and was looking forward to.  However over the past year they've taken a step back from pursuing commercial work and pursuing more multi-family and more-so just mixed-use projects for commercial work.  Which is Ok for the most part, they have a plan for him still and he doesn't care too much about the job he's building, more people and process management but he really enjoys the variety in commercial construction and our opportunity is exciting with the growth and process refinement and of course the leadership aspect. He has a screening call that HR is in the process of setting up with a place he applied to earlier in the week but he's only applied to that one role so far. He and his team will be in Ft. Lauderdale next week but he'll be flexible next Friday for a video call. Let me know if we can set something up with on Friday. Thanks! emails.subject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully.
emails.bodyText:
Subject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully.
Date: 2026-05-16T15:26:54Z
From: Verizon <communications@b2binfo.verizonwireless.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges We are writing to inform you of an important upcoming network update regarding the International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) ranges used for new Internet of Things (IoT) and Fixed Wireless Access (FWA) device activations on the Verizon network. Due to high demand, we are nearing the depletion of the 311-480 IMSI range, which is what most activations on the Verizon network use today. New SIM cards and eSIM profile assignments will be provisioned with 311-270 IMSIs as the 311-480 range nears exhaustion. Your existing, currently active devices will continue to function normally . Additionally, if you have an inventory of SIMs that have 311-480 IMSI assignments, you should be able to use them in your future device deployments. Certain SIM SKUs have a limited lifespan and will stop working if they remain unactivated for a certain number of years. Contact your Verizon point of contact to determine what limitations apply to your SIM SKU inventory. You may need to take immediate action to prevent potential issues with new activations of existing deployments and any upcoming deployments on alternative IMSI ranges. If your devices or management systems are hardcoded to exclusively recognize the 311-480 range, new activation devices with the 311-270 range or another range could fail to activate or may cause functionality issues. Please share this information immediately with your internal technical teams, device manufacturers, and module vendors to ensure all firmware, device software, and backend systems are updated to recognize the new Verizon IMSI ranges that are being commercialized (including 311-270). Please also ensure that your SIM procurement is updated to support SIMs with alternative IMSI ranges. We encourage you to begin work immediately to support the new IMSI ranges to avoid disruption to your business operations. Please work with your Verizon point of contact, or use the link below for the latest updates on this topic, to determine the appropriate IMSI assignment and, therefore, the new SIM SKU (UICC or eUICC) for your existing and/or new deployments. For more details, please visit our “what’s new” page here . Thanks for being a Verizon customer. © 2026 Verizon This email was sent to BCLYMER@ALLEATOGROUP.COM . We respect your privacy. Please review our Privacy Policy . You can unsubscribe at any time or update your wireless communication options at the preference center . Verizon, One Verizon Way, Mail Code: 180WVB, Basking Ridge, NJ 07920 B-xxxx

Please note that the entrance to the hotel can be accessed from Dong Khoi Street, Time Square Building, 57-69F Dong Khoi Street, District 1. messages.subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Interview Follow Up - Jon Knapp
Date: 2026-05-18T16:19:04Z
From: Jonathon Knapp <jonathon.knapp@icloud.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

You don't often get email from jonathon.knapp@icloud.com. Learn why this is important Brandon, Thank you again for taking the time to connect this morning. I appreciated the opportunity to learn more about your vision for the company and the direction the team is heading. I also appreciated the direct conversation around design-build experience. While my background has been rooted more heavily in large-scale renovation, exterior construction, and operational leadership, I believe many of the same fundamentals translate - building strong teams, driving accountability, managing risk, and executing work efficiently at scale. Additionally, my reconstruction and remediation background has provided extensive exposure to building failures, envelope issues, and long-term performance concerns. I believe that perspective can bring value to new construction environments by helping identify and mitigate risk early in the process, while also supporting any future expansion into reconstruction or renovation-related work. I appreciate your time and consideration and enjoyed the conversation. Thank you, Jon Knapp 303.918.2540

Subject: RE: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1,  Revit Models
Date: 2026-05-18T15:12:54Z
From: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>
To: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>, Vinod Ravikumar <vinod.ravikumar@exol.com>, Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>, Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>, Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>, Bob  Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>, Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>, Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>, Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>, Robert Snedeker <rsnedeker@symbotic.com>

You don't often get email from wblank@symbotic.com. Learn why this is important Hello Steve,   Please find the reaction data attached.  I’ll post them on the SharePoint drive also.    The column separation is 4“x4” lateral and 5.3” vertical.   Spanning between the slab control joints is typical.     WILLIAM BLANK | senior project engineer e: wblank@symbotic.com www.symbotic.com SYMBOTIC 200 Research Drive Wilmington, MA 01887 Reinvent the warehouse | Reimagine the supply chain® **Symbotic Confidential** This e-mail and any attachments contain Symbotic confidential information and are for the sole use of the intended recipient(s). Unauthorized review, use, or disclosure is prohibited. If you are not the intended recipient, please contact the sender by reply e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message.   From: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com> Sent: Friday, May 15, 2026 2:56 PM To: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>; Vinod Ravikumar <vinod.ravikumar@exol.com>; Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>; Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>; Bob Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>; Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>; Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>; Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>; Robert Snedeker <rsnedeker@symbotic.com> Cc: Connor Brulotte <cbrulotte@symbotic.com>; Mark Jodoin <mjodoin@symbotic.com>; Donald Seifert <dseifert@symbotic.com>; Michael Cole <mcole@symbotic.com>; Alex Calder <acalder@symbotic.com>; Andrew Grandin <agrandin@symbotic.com>; Parker Asmus <parker.asmus@exol.com> Subject: RE: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1, Revit Models   CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the Symbotic organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content. Please report any suspicious emails to Symbotic IT Helpdesk.   Hello All,   I just got off a call with the Morrisville landlord to review our first phase of plans. They have a concern regarding the pad that is located in close proximity to column K19 based on how close it would be to the footer for that building column. @SYM team Is there a distance we typically try and keep away from these columns for concrete work, this does look very tight?   @Jerome Daksiewicz @Bob Wright @Brandon Clymer we need to have the structural engineer look at this more closely.   I also want to understand if we feel this is a fully inclusive list of pads needed for the slab. There doesn’t appear to be that many compared to some previous projects. Do we feel comfortable that this is all-inclusive or think there could be more coming?   DKGR/Alleato Team, have we assessed some of the areas called out on the pits&pads drawing to be confirmed by EOR (ex. #9 to be confirmed as not needed) We also NEED the reactions details for these ASAP – the landlord basically said there is nothing for them to review without having the structural reactions for these. @William Blank @Robert Snedeker what is the ETA for these? Finally, they did call out that some of these pits & pads locations appear to span across control joints in the slab and suggested trying to avoid that. I said I would pass it along, but told them this is most likely more difficult and less flexible than the column footer interference. There are less control joints on this slab being a Trilytics Trislab, but they called out that because there are less control joints, there is potentially more of a shift on them.   System Designs — Trilytics   We should probably discuss on a call early next week.   Steve Fischer Senior Director, Facilities M: +1 610-764-3984 The Robotic Logistics Platform ™ built for tomorrow. | www.exol.com   From: William Blank < wblank@symbotic.com > Sent: Thursday, May 7, 2026 10:22 AM To: Steve Fischer < steve.fischer@exol.com >; Vinod Ravikumar < vinod.ravikumar@greenboxlogistics.com >; Scott Thomas < scott.thomas@greenboxlogistics.com >; Brandon Clymer < bclymer@alleatogroup.com >; Jesse Dawson < jdawson@alleatogroup.com >; Bob Wright < bwright@alleatogroup.com >; Parker Hollingsworth < phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com >; Jerome Daksiewicz < jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com >; Heather Jutras < heather.jutras@exol.com > Cc: Robert Snedeker < rsnedeker@symbotic.com >; Connor Brulotte < cbrulotte@symbotic.com >; Mark Jodoin < mjodoin@symbotic.com >; Donald Seifert < dseifert@symbotic.com >; Michael Cole < mcole@symbotic.com >; Alex Calder < acalder@symbotic.com >; Andrew Grandin < agrandin@symbotic.com >; Parker Asmus < parker.asmus@exol.com > Subject: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1, Revit Models   Hello All, The below files have been uploaded to the SharePoint Site 4.01.3 - Symbotic Revit WILLIAM BLANK | senior project engineer e: wblank@symbotic.com ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ NkdkJdXPPEBannerStart External Sender - From: (William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>) This message came from outside your organization.   NkdkJdXPPEBannerEnd Hello All, The below files have been uploaded to the SharePoint Site     4.01.3 - Symbotic Revit   WILLIAM BLANK | senior project engineer e: wblank@symbotic.com www.symbotic.com SYMBOTIC 200 Research Drive Wilmington, MA 01887 Reinvent the warehouse | Reimagine the supply chain® **Symbotic Confidential** This e-mail and any attachments contain Symbotic confidential information and are for the sole use of the intended recipient(s). Do we feel comfortable that this is all-inclusive or think there could be more coming?   DKGR/Alleato Team, have we assessed some of the areas called out on the pits&pads drawing to be confirmed by EOR (ex. #9 to be confirmed as not needed) We also NEED the reactions details for these ASAP – the landlord basically said there is nothing for them to review without having the structural reactions for these. @William Blank @Robert Snedeker what is the ETA for these? Finally, they did call out that some of these pits & pads locations appear to span across control joints in the slab and suggested trying to avoid that. I said I would pass it along, but told them this is most likely more difficult and less flexible than the column footer interference. There are less control joints on this slab being a Trilytics Trislab, but they called out that because there are less control joints, there is potentially more of a shift on them.   System Designs — Trilytics   We should probably discuss on a call early next week.   Steve Fischer Senior Director, Facilities M: +1 610-764-3984 The Robotic Logistics Platform ™ built for tomorrow. | www.exol.com   From: William Blank < wblank@symbotic.com > Sent: Thursday, May 7, 2026 10:22 AM To: Steve Fischer < steve.fischer@exol.com >; Subject: RE: Exol, Morrisville, PA, Phase 1,  Revit Models
Date: 2026-05-15T18:56:29Z
From: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>
To: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com>, Vinod Ravikumar <vinod.ravikumar@exol.com>, Scott Thomas <scott.thomas@exol.com>, Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>, Jesse Dawson <jdawson@alleatogroup.com>, Bob  Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>, Parker Hollingsworth <phollingsworth@alleatogroup.com>, Jerome Daksiewicz <jerome.daksiewicz@dkgrar.com>, Heather Jutras <heather.jutras@exol.com>, Robert Snedeker <rsnedeker@symbotic.com>

Hello All,   I just got off a call with the Morrisville landlord to review our first phase of plans. They have a concern regarding the pad that is located in close proximity to column K19 based on how close it would be to the footer for that building column. @SYM team Is there a distance we typically try and keep away from these columns for concrete work, this does look very tight?   @Jerome Daksiewicz @Bob Wright @Brandon Clymer we need to have the structural engineer look at this more closely.   I also want to understand if we feel this is a fully inclusive list of pads needed for the slab. There doesn’t appear to be that many compared to some previous projects. Do we feel comfortable that this is all-inclusive or think there could be more coming?   DKGR/Alleato Team, have we assessed some of the areas called out on the pits&pads drawing to be confirmed by EOR (ex. #9 to be confirmed as not needed) We also NEED the reactions details for these ASAP – the landlord basically said there is nothing for them to review without having the structural reactions for these. @William Blank @Robert Snedeker what is the ETA for these? Finally, they did call out that some of these pits & pads locations appear to span across control joints in the slab and suggested trying to avoid that. I said I would pass it along, but told them this is most likely more difficult and less flexible than the column footer interference. There are less control joints on this slab being a Trilytics Trislab, but they called out that because there are less control joints, there is potentially more of a shift on them.   System Designs — Trilytics   We should probably discuss on a call early next week.   Steve Fischer Senior Director, Facilities M: +1 610-764-3984 The Robotic Logistics Platform ™ built for tomorrow. | www.exol.com   From: William Blank <wblank@symbotic.com> Sent: Thursday, May 7, 2026 10:22 AM To: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>; messages.subject: Re: Hillsdale-Alleato A141
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Re: Hillsdale-Alleato A141
Date: 2026-05-14T17:43:38Z
From: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>
To: Logan Stevens <Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com>

See below.  Brandon Clymer CEO at Alleato Group  Mobile   317.760.0088   |   Email   bclymer@alleatogroup.com Web   www.alleatogroup.com     |         Indianapolis - 8383 Craig Street, Suite 150 Indianapolis, IN 46250  Tampa/St Pete - 701 94th Avenue North, Suite 118, St. Petersburg, FL 33702     From:  Logan Stevens <Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com> Sent:  Thursday, May 14, 2026 1:27 PM To:  Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Subject:  FW: Hillsdale-Alleato A141   Clymer,   I discussed with Grant this morning and he and I worked through some comments and questions. I think we are much closer. They accepted several of our changes, rejected some, and tweaked a few. PDFs attached show the modifications a bit more easily than the word versions they sent.   A141 – significant changes: 3.14 –They agreed to the $150k number for their approval of subs and to tying the rejections to the material concerns we detailed. They rejected the last sentence about the increase to contract sum and contract time, claiming these figures should be known at time of approval. I.e. if they reject and you have to select someone else, they claim this should be built into the revised contract sum prior to commencement. -  If we are going to contract now with our proposal then any changes they decide wouldn't be accounted for, if they reject the subs we have selected then we can't be on the hook for that.  3.1.14.1 – Indemnification. They pushed back on limiting to “third-party” claims in the event injury to the owner. They removed limiting language to the performance of the work  and tied to any negligent acts or omissions or Architect, Consultant, or a Contractor. - I defer to your judgement.  6.2.3 – they added back the waiver of increase in contract sum or contract time if work begins before a change order. They claim you should be consulting with them first and don’t want to end up in a dispute. I asked instead for a carveout that you can continue to the extent needed for emergencies – they added language to that effect that you can continue before consent if needed to prevent bodily injury or property damage. - I am fine with this if we can get them to bend on 3.14.  9.3.1.2.1 – reduced the lien waivers for 1 st  tier subs/suppliers to $50k vs $150k. - Meet in the middle at 100k.  9.5.1 – on disputed payments, they tweaked the language as we were anticipating. If they withhold and were not entitled to do so, you will get an equitable adjustment to time and $. I think this is fair middle ground. -  Agree 9.5.2 – rather than 30 days after submission of the application for payment, they revised to say 45 days after issuance of certificate for payment you can stop work. Exhibit A revised to align. Agree 9.10.1 – they revised the last sentence to say the project should be delivered fully complete and functional with no punch list items for final completion. - Defer to your judgment.  10.3.4 – for liability arising from hazardous materials, they pushed back on many of our carveouts but did agree to expand to any their responsibility for their separate contractors I’m happy to push back if you have concerns about any of these. Let me know if you want to discuss. - I am okay with this, we shouldn't have any hazardous materials.    Logan  Stevens ,  Associate ​ ​ ​ ​ Mobile: (574) 606-6689   |   Direct: (260) 425-4742 |  Email: Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com 888 S. Harrison Street Suite 600, Fort Wayne, IN 46802 Atlanta | Baltimore | Boston | Chicago | Dallas | Delaware | Denver | Florida | Indiana | Los Angeles | Michigan | Minneapolis Nashville | New Jersey | New York | Ohio | Philadelphia | Phoenix | Raleigh | Salt Lake City | San Diego | Washington, D.C.   From:  Schertzing, Grant E. <schertzingg@millerjohnson.com> Sent:  Thursday, May 14, 2026 10:22 AM To:  Logan Stevens <Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com> Cc:  Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Mark Ferderer <mferderer@ferdimation.com> Subject:  [EXTERNAL] RE: Hillsdale-Alleato A141   Logan, Thank you for talking through a few points this morning. messages.subject: FW: Hillsdale-Alleato A141
messages.bodyText:
Subject: FW: Hillsdale-Alleato A141
Date: 2026-05-14T17:27:57Z
From: Logan Stevens <Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Clymer,   I discussed with Grant this morning and he and I worked through some comments and questions. I think we are much closer. They accepted several of our changes, rejected some, and tweaked a few. PDFs attached show the modifications a bit more easily than the word versions they sent.   A141 – significant changes: 3.14 –They agreed to the $150k number for their approval of subs and to tying the rejections to the material concerns we detailed. They rejected the last sentence about the increase to contract sum and contract time, claiming these figures should be known at time of approval. I.e. if they reject and you have to select someone else, they claim this should be built into the revised contract sum prior to commencement. 3.1.14.1 – Indemnification. They pushed back on limiting to “third-party” claims in the event injury to the owner. They removed limiting language to the performance of the work  and tied to any negligent acts or omissions or Architect, Consultant, or a Contractor. 6.2.3 – they added back the waiver of increase in contract sum or contract time if work begins before a change order. They claim you should be consulting with them first and don’t want to end up in a dispute. I asked instead for a carveout that you can continue to the extent needed for emergencies – they added language to that effect that you can continue before consent if needed to prevent bodily injury or property damage. 9.3.1.2.1 – reduced the lien waivers for 1 st tier subs/suppliers to $50k vs $150k. 9.5.1 – on disputed payments, they tweaked the language as we were anticipating. If they withhold and were not entitled to do so, you will get an equitable adjustment to time and $. I think this is fair middle ground. 9.5.2 – rather than 30 days after submission of the application for payment, they revised to say 45 days after issuance of certificate for payment you can stop work. Exhibit A revised to align. 9.10.1 – they revised the last sentence to say the project should be delivered fully complete and functional with no punch list items for final completion. 10.3.4 – for liability arising from hazardous materials, they pushed back on many of our carveouts but did agree to expand to any their responsibility for their separate contractors I’m happy to push back if you have concerns about any of these. Let me know if you want to discuss.   Logan  Stevens ,  Associate ​ ​ ​ ​ Mobile:  (574) 606-6689   |   Direct:  (260) 425-4742 |  Email:  Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com 888 S. Harrison Street Suite 600, Fort Wayne, IN 46802 Atlanta | Baltimore | Boston | Chicago | Dallas | Delaware | Denver | Florida | Indiana | Los Angeles | Michigan | Minneapolis Nashville | New Jersey | New York | Ohio | Philadelphia | Phoenix | Raleigh | Salt Lake City | San Diego | Washington, D.C.   From: Schertzing, Grant E. <schertzingg@millerjohnson.com> Sent: Thursday, May 14, 2026 10:22 AM To: Logan Stevens <Logan.Stevens@btlaw.com> Cc: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>; Mark Ferderer <mferderer@ferdimation.com> Subject: [EXTERNAL] RE: Hillsdale-Alleato A141   Logan, Thank you for talking through a few points this morning. messages.bodyText:
Subject: HGCC | Pro's Corner, May 16
Date: 2026-05-17T20:00:41Z
From: The Hawthorns Golf and Country Club <padams@hawthornscountryclub.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Can't read or see images? View this email in a browser. RESCHEDULED: Hawthorns 500 Join us on Saturday May 30th for the first Saturday Men’s Club event of the season with the Hawthorns 500 Flag tournament.  Please see the flyer below for all the details and click the link to get registered. REGISTER ▶ Callaway Fitting Day Callaway will be here at Hawthorns for a fitting day on Wednesday May 20th from 2:00-6:00 with the new Quantum product.  Schedule your fitting below to upgrade your equipment for the season! REGISTER ▶ Titleist GTS Fitting Day Titleist will be here on Tuesday June 9 th for a fitting day with the brand-new GTS Metal release.  This will be a great opportunity to test the newest driver on the market for 2026.  Over 50 tour players have switched to the GTS models.  Click the link to book your fitting! REGISTER ▶ Pro’s Fore Clothes With the month of May comes our annual trade-in trade-up golf shirt sale! From now until the end of the month we are looking to donate as much clothing as possible to the Pros Fore Clothes Foundation! The Pros Fore Clothes Foundation is a 501(c)(3) non-profit foundation that provides clothing to people in need within the United States and around the world. This foundation has donated over 350,000 pounds of clothing and The Hawthorns Golf Staff is proud to be an integral part of their success! Please see below for pricing and promotional details. Donate 1 shirt and receive 15% off 1 new shirt in stock Donate 2 shirts and receive 20% off 2 new shirts in stock Donate 3 shirts and receive 25% off 3 new shirts in stock *The sale is also eligible for shorts and pants!   Junior Golf June Sessions Our Hawthorns Junior Golf June sessions are right around the corner with Little Linksters for ages 3-5 and the Junior Development Program for ages 6-12 starting on Tuesday June 9 th .  Please see the flyer below for all the details and click the link to register your child.  REGISTER FOR LITTLE LINKSTERS REGISTER FOR JUNIOR PROGRAM Ladies Wednesday Evening 9 & Wine Ladies, please join us this season on Wednesday evenings starting on May 20 th for a fun night on the golf course!  We will have wine outside the golf shop from 5:15-5:45 p.m. and then shotgun on the course from 5:45-7:45 p.m.  We encourage everyone to stay after the round and join the group in the Players Lounge Area for drinks, dinner, and social period.  This is a great way to learn the game and meet other ladies in a FUN, stress free environment! You can make your own pairings each week, just be sure to register by 12:00 p.m. each Wednesday.  If you do not have a group, the golf shop will be happy to pair you up!  You will find links to each week on the Calendar of the Hawthorns Website. CLICK HERE REGISTER 2026 Golf Schedule There are many exciting events on the schedule for 2026!  You can also find the golf events on the calendar of the Hawthorns website. CLICK HERE TO DOWNLOAD THE 2026 GOLF SCHEDULE Fairways & Greens, Philip Adams, PGA Director of Golf The Hawthorns Golf & Country Club The Hawthorns Golf & Country Club 12255 Club Point Drive Fishers, IN 46037 317.845.9100 | www.hawthornscountryclub.com Unsubscribe from Hawthorns Communications

Lauderdale next week but he'll be flexible next Friday for a video call. Let me know if we can set something up with on Friday. Thanks! threads.latestSubject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully.
threads.latestPreview: Subject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully. Date: 2026-05-16T15:26:54Z From: Verizon <communications@b2binfo.verizonwireless.com> To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges We are writing to inform you of an

messages.subject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully.
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges. Please read carefully.
Date: 2026-05-16T15:26:54Z
From: Verizon <communications@b2binfo.verizonwireless.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Important update on Verizon IMSI ranges We are writing to inform you of an important upcoming network update regarding the International Mobile Subscriber Identity (IMSI) ranges used for new Internet of Things (IoT) and Fixed Wireless Access (FWA) device activations on the Verizon network. Due to high demand, we are nearing the depletion of the 311-480 IMSI range, which is what most activations on the Verizon network use today. New SIM cards and eSIM profile assignments will be provisioned with 311-270 IMSIs as the 311-480 range nears exhaustion. Your existing, currently active devices will continue to function normally . Additionally, if you have an inventory of SIMs that have 311-480 IMSI assignments, you should be able to use them in your future device deployments. Certain SIM SKUs have a limited lifespan and will stop working if they remain unactivated for a certain number of years. Contact your Verizon point of contact to determine what limitations apply to your SIM SKU inventory. You may need to take immediate action to prevent potential issues with new activations of existing deployments and any upcoming deployments on alternative IMSI ranges. If your devices or management systems are hardcoded to exclusively recognize the 311-480 range, new activation devices with the 311-270 range or another range could fail to activate or may cause functionality issues. Please share this information immediately with your internal technical teams, device manufacturers, and module vendors to ensure all firmware, device software, and backend systems are updated to recognize the new Verizon IMSI ranges that are being commercialized (including 311-270). Please also ensure that your SIM procurement is updated to support SIMs with alternative IMSI ranges. We encourage you to begin work immediately to support the new IMSI ranges to avoid disruption to your business operations. Please work with your Verizon point of contact, or use the link below for the latest updates on this topic, to determine the appropriate IMSI assignment and, therefore, the new SIM SKU (UICC or eUICC) for your existing and/or new deployments. messages.subject: Collaboration update
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Collaboration update
Date: 2026-05-16T00:59:33Z
From: tony@ibexdevgroup.com tony@ibexdevgroup.com <tony@ibexdevgroup.com>
To: Mariusz Łach <mlach@bp2.eu>, Katarzyna <kcwiklinska@bp2.eu>, Grzegorz Bizoń <gbizon@bp2.eu>

Team,    I wanted to provide an update on this week's activities: Software training: Phil, Russ, and I attended a great introduction led by Paul on Monday. We understand he is working on the imperial conversion of the quoting software, and we will begin testing it out to gain better familiarity. Product development/certification: Phil and I met with Slawo on Wednesday to continue planning the product certification. We are rapidly developing options from multiple sources and should have some solid updates very soon. We have established a regular Wednesday morning meeting to ensure we rapidly move towards a solution and keep all communication flowing. Marketing plan: Phil and I began working on our marketing strategy this week. Once we complete the first draft, we will send it out to gain feedback. Working with your team this week has been an absolute pleasure. They are extremely competent, kind, and professional. We remain as excited as ever about the future. Talk soon,  -Tony

threads.latestPreview: Subject: Major update from Message center Date: 2026-05-15T23:27:30Z From: Microsoft 365 Message center <o365mc@microsoft.com> To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> View the description of the update.           &nbsp

messages.subject: Major update from Message center
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Major update from Message center
Date: 2026-05-15T23:27:30Z
From: Microsoft 365 Message center <o365mc@microsoft.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

View the description of the update.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Security Detection Report in Teams Admin Center MC1311977 · ALLEATO GROUP Tenant ID: 4998a178-5591-4354-811e-d0d6c7994f75 Introduction A new Security Detection Report in the Teams admin center provides a unified view of messaging security detections across signals such as impersonation, malicious URLs, and weaponizable file types. This centralized reporting experience improves visibility into threats in Teams and helps admins investigate and respond to suspicious activity more efficiently. This message is associated with Microsoft 365 Roadmap ID 560702 . When this will happen: General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout will begin in late June 2026 and is expected to complete by late June 2026 . How this affects your organization: Who is affected: Admins who manage Microsoft Teams environments and security operations Security and helpdesk teams responsible for investigating messaging threats What will happen: A new Security Detection Report will be available in the Teams admin center under Analytics & reports > Protection reports > Security detections report. Screenshot: Example of a Security Detection Report: The report provides centralized visibility into messaging security detections across Teams, including impersonation attempts, malicious links, and unsafe file types. Admins can review detection details such as: Sender Recipient context Detection type Available user actions Admins can export report data for further investigation, including additional metadata such as sender identifier and thread ID. The following messaging security protections are enabled by default: Impersonation detection does not require configuration. Malicious link scanning can be managed through Messaging safety settings in the Teams admin center. Unsafe file type scanning can be managed through Messaging safety settings in the Teams admin center. Admins can block malicious external users identified in the report through External access settings to prevent further communication attempts. What you can do to prepare: Review current Messaging safety settings to confirm malicious link and file scanning configurations. Familiarize security and helpdesk teams with the new report and export capabilities. Update investigation and response workflows to incorporate Teams detection signals. Use Teams admin center navigation path to locate the report: Analytics & reports > Protection reports > Security detections report Update internal documentation or runbooks if you document security investigation processes. Before rollout, we will update this post with new documentation. Compliance considerations: Introduces enhanced admin reporting for monitoring and investigating messaging security detections. View in the Microsoft 365 admin center Did you find this email helpful? Yes No You're subscribed to this email using bclymer@alleatogroup.com. If you're an IT admin, you're subscribed by default, but you can unsubscribe at any time . If you're not an IT admin, ask your admin to remove your email address from Microsoft 365 message center preferences. How to view translated messages Privacy Statement Microsoft Corporation, One Microsoft Way, ​Redmond, WA 98052​

threads.latestPreview: Subject: Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner - Indianapolis 500 Edition Date: 2026-05-15T18:28:31Z From: Kurt Luidhardt <kurt@prospergroupcorp.com> To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com> Brandon: I hope you're doing well  We should catch up soon.  Was thinking of y

messages.subject: Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner - Indianapolis 500 Edition
messages.bodyText:
Subject: Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner - Indianapolis 500 Edition
Date: 2026-05-15T18:28:31Z
From: Kurt Luidhardt <kurt@prospergroupcorp.com>
To: Brandon Clymer <bclymer@alleatogroup.com>

Brandon: I hope you're doing well  We should catch up soon.  Was thinking of you for this dinner we're doing Saturday night before the Indy500.  Should be some great people there - Tiger21 members, UHNW donors, etc.  More info below: Kristen and I have been building a private introduction and deal flow network called the Liberty Builders Exchange. Periodically we host dinners for our network as a way to facilitate in-person introductions AND as a way to introduce new people to what we’re doing. We’ll be holding one of those dinners in Indianapolis the night before the Indy500 which will include part of the 65 guests that we will be having there + some cool local people like you. It will be at Weber Grill downtown, Saturday May 23rd. What: Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner Who is this for? Any businessperson who values connections and understands that the right relationships, particularly with aligned leaders, can exponentially transform their outcomes. People who love making connections/giving to others are especially welcome. From our event in Dallas in March, we currently know of three business deals already in discussion totaling over $3.5 million in business. Logistics/Information: Saturday, May 23rd at 630pm Weber Grill (10 N Illinois St) Expect 2.5 hours for the dinner. We should have about 12-15 total attend. We'll be facilitating conversations at your table with other businesspersons, hoping to build high value relationships for everyone involved! The evening will end with everyone getting a chance to make an ASK of the group and, more importantly, offer up their connections. Knowing you, I think you'd be a great addition to the event. We'd love to have you. It is $150 to attend and includes the meal. Click Join us for a Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner to RSVP and pay. Make sure you select the dinner in Indianapolis as there are other options! Also, anyone else you recommend that we invite? Even if you cannot come, a recommendation would be valuable. I'm really looking forward to seeing you! Kurt Link here: Join us for a Liberty Builders Exchange Dinner P.S. A lot of our guests will be attending the race too. One of America's 25 fastest race car drivers, Sting Ray Robb, is part of our Liberty Builders Exchange Network. If you'd like a really cool behind-the-scenes experience for the race let us know. Kurt   Luidhardt Co-Founder The Prosper Group 317-886-4438 | 765-425-4980 kurt@prospergroupcorp.com https://www.prospergroupcorp.com/ 4495 W Stones Crossing Road, Greenwood, IN 46143
